heading: Membership in WTO of boycotting countries
label: 3553 Membership in WTO of boycotting countries
text: It is the sense of the Congress that the Trade Representative should vigorously oppose the admission into the World Trade Organization of any country which, through its laws, regulations, official policies, or governmental practices, fosters, imposes, complies with, furthers, or supports any boycott described in (as in effect on ), including requiring or encouraging entities within that country to refuse to do business with persons who do not comply with requests to take any action prohibited under that section.section 4607(a) of title 50August 20, 1994
notes: References in Text(as in effect on ), referred to in text, means , War and National Defense, prior to its repeal by , , .Section 4607(a) of title 50section 4607(a) of Title 50Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title XVII, § 1766(a)132 Stat. 2232August 20, 1994Aug. 13, 2018
